item.title: China Adds More Japanese Entities to Export Control List
item.description: The Chinese commerce ministry said it added 20 Japanese entities to an export control list that prohibits Chinese firms from selling dual-use products to those companies

item.title: The Graduate-School Dropout Toppling China’s Academic Stars
item.description: Shamed by a blogger, Chinese universities are waging a blitzkrieg against academic fraud.

item.title: China Manufacturing Gauge Shows Slower Growth in Activity
item.description: A private gauge showed China’s manufacturing activity grew at a slower pace in June due to tapered factory production, diverging from a pickup shown in a competing index.

item.title: Beyond Politics: A China-Born Journalist’s American Story
item.description: Plus, a professor’s lesson: The First Amendment favors verifying facts over accepting government claims

item.title: China’s Manufacturing Activity Expands, Buoyed by Exports
item.description: The data suggest the world’s second-largest economy continues to be supported by strong overseas demand for Chinese goods amid tepid domestic demand.

item.title: Guo Wengui, China Critic and Tycoon, Sentenced to 30 Years for Fraud
item.description: After conviction in a U.S. court, the Trump administration faces calls to pardon or deport the Beijing gadfly who built a devoted following.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
